lines.en_lit: and shall leave them there” teaches that require storing away, Rabbi Dosa says: they are acceptable for a common priest. And what, the verse states: “And shall leave them there,” that may not use them subsequent Yom Kippur
What, is it not with regard to this that disagree: One Sage, holds is a service; and one Sage, holds it is not a service,
not everyone is service and here they disagree about this: One Sage, holds a verse is necessary include And one Sage, holds that a verse is not necessary.

Rabbi Avin raised a dilemma: How much removal of the ashes? Do we derive it from the teruma of the tithe, or do we derive it from the donations Midian, where Come hear As Rabbi Ḥiyya taught it is stated here: “And he shall take up and it is said elsewhere, “And he shall take up Just as there, a handful so too, here, a handful

Rav said: there are four services a non-priest is liable to death Sprinkling and burning and the water libation and the wine libation And Levi said: also the removal of the ashes. And similarly, Levi taught in his baraitot: The removal of ashes also

What is the reason for Rav’s As it is written: “And you and your sons with you shall keep your priesthood in everything pertaining to the altar and to that within the veil; and you shall serve; I give you the priesthood as a service of gift; and the common man that draws near shall be put to death” “A service of gift” and not a service removal “And you shall serve [va’avadtem]” a service that is complete [avoda tamma] and not a service that has another service after it
And Levi? the Merciful One includes “in everything pertaining to the altar,” And Rav that to include the seven sprinklings that are inside and of the leper,
And Levi He derives from “pertaining “everything pertaining” And Rav not derive “pertaining “everything pertaining

But say “In everything pertaining” generalization, “a service of gift” specification, a generalization a specification. the generalization includes only what in the specification. A service of giving, yes, a service of removal not the verse states:
